# ================================================================
# BankApp — Security Scanning Pipeline
#
# TOOLS COVERED:
#   1. Semgrep      — SAST (static analysis of Java source code)
#   2. SonarQube    — Code quality + security (self-hosted, free)
#   3. Trivy        — Container + filesystem vuln scanning
#   4. OWASP DC     — Dependency vulnerability audit (CVE database)
#   5. TFLint       — Terraform linting + AWS best practice rules
#   6. Checkov      — Terraform IaC security & compliance scanning
#
# ADVANCED CONCEPTS USED:
#   - Each tool in its own job (parallel execution, clear failure isolation)
#   - SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab (all tools feed one dashboard)
#   - Checkov/TFLint only scan when terraform/ changes (path filtering)
#   - Semgrep/OWASP only scan when src/ changes (path filtering)
#   - Severity thresholds that BLOCK the pipeline (not just warn)
#   - Baseline/suppression files for known accepted risks
#   - SonarQube Quality Gate as a blocking step
#   - Final gate job — nothing deploys if any scan fails
#
# HOW TO RUN LOCALLY (zero cost):
#   # Semgrep
#   docker run --rm -v $(pwd):/src semgrep/semgrep semgrep scan --config=auto src/
#
#   # Trivy
#   trivy fs . --severity HIGH,CRITICAL
#
#   # TFLint
#   cd terraform && tflint --recursive
#
#   # Checkov
#   checkov -d terraform/ --framework terraform
#
#   # OWASP Dependency Check
#   mvn org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:check
# ================================================================

name: Security Scanning Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop, "release/**"]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 2 * * *"       # nightly full scan at 2 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

# Cancel duplicate runs on the same branch
concurrency:
  group: security-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  JAVA_VERSION: "21"
  SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ vars.SONAR_HOST_URL }}    # stored as repo variable, not secret

# ================================================================
# JOB 1 — SEMGREP (SAST — Java source code analysis)
# ================================================================
jobs:
  semgrep:
    name: "Semgrep SAST"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      contents: read
      security-events: write    # upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab

    # Only run when Java source changes — saves CI minutes
    # Exception: always run on main/release and scheduled scans
    # (on: push filtering applies at workflow level; this handles PR path filtering)

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0        # full history for blame info in findings

      # ADVANCED: Semgrep — runs your source through 2000+ rules
      # Rules cover: OWASP Top 10, security hotspots, Java-specific issues
      # Free for open source; Semgrep Pro has even more rules
      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/java                         
            p/spring-framework             
            p/owasp-top-ten               
            p/secrets                      
            p/sql-injection               
          generateSarif: "1"
          # ADVANCED: publishToken enables Semgrep Cloud dashboard
          # Leave empty for fully local/free usage
          publishToken: ${{ secrets.SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN }}

      # Upload findings to GitHub Security > Code scanning tab
      - name: Upload Semgrep SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: semgrep.sarif
          category: semgrep

  # ================================================================
  # JOB 2 — SONARQUBE (code quality + security)
  # ================================================================
  sonarqube:
    name: "SonarQube Analysis"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # SonarQube needs test results and coverage to give meaningful output
    # Run independently — don't block on semgrep

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0        # SonarQube needs full git history for blame

      - name: Set up JDK
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
          distribution: temurin

      # Cache Maven + SonarQube analysis cache — speeds up scanner significantly
      - name: Cache Maven + Sonar packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.m2/repository
            ~/.sonar/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-maven-sonar-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-maven-sonar-
            ${{ runner.os }}-maven-

      # Build + run tests to generate coverage data for SonarQube
      - name: Build and test (generate coverage)
        run: mvn verify -B -Dspring.profiles.active=test

      # ADVANCED: SonarQube scan
      # SONAR_HOST_URL points to your self-hosted SonarQube
      # For local practice: run SonarQube in Docker (see HOW-TO-RUN.md)
      - name: SonarQube Scan
        env:
          SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          mvn sonar:sonar \
            -Dsonar.projectKey=bankapp \
            -Dsonar.projectName="BankApp" \
            -Dsonar.host.url=${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }} \
            -Dsonar.token=${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }} \
            -Dsonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths=target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml \
            -Dsonar.java.binaries=target/classes \
            -Dsonar.qualitygate.wait=true \
            -Dsonar.qualitygate.timeout=300

      # ADVANCED: Quality Gate check — this is what makes SonarQube BLOCK deploys
      # qualitygate.wait=true above already blocks, but this adds explicit output
      - name: Check SonarQube Quality Gate
        env:
          SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          STATUS=$(curl -s -u "$SONAR_TOKEN:" \
            "${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}/api/qualitygates/project_status?projectKey=bankapp" \
            | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['projectStatus']['status'])")

          echo "Quality Gate Status: $STATUS"

          if [ "$STATUS" != "OK" ]; then
            echo "SonarQube Quality Gate FAILED — blocking deployment"
            echo "View issues: ${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}/dashboard?id=bankapp"
            exit 1
          fi

  # ================================================================
  # JOB 3 — TRIVY (container + filesystem scanning)
  # ================================================================
  trivy:
    name: "Trivy Vulnerability Scan"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      contents: read
      security-events: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # ── Scan 1: filesystem (source + deps before building image) ──
      - name: Trivy — filesystem scan
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: fs
          scan-ref: "."
          format: sarif
          output: trivy-fs.sarif
          severity: CRITICAL,HIGH
          exit-code: "1"        # BLOCKS pipeline on findings
          ignore-unfixed: true  # skip vulns with no fix available
          # ADVANCED: custom ignore rules for known false-positives
          trivyignores: .trivyignore

      - name: Upload filesystem SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: trivy-fs.sarif
          category: trivy-filesystem

      # ── Scan 2: build image then scan the actual image ──
      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Build image for scanning (don't push)
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: false
          tags: bankapp:scan-${{ github.sha }}
          load: true            # load into local Docker daemon for scanning
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

      - name: Trivy — container image scan
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: image
          image-ref: bankapp:scan-${{ github.sha }}
          format: sarif
          output: trivy-image.sarif
          severity: CRITICAL,HIGH
          exit-code: "1"
          ignore-unfixed: true

      - name: Upload image SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: trivy-image.sarif
          category: trivy-image

      # ── Scan 3: Terraform IaC scan with Trivy ──
      # Trivy can also scan IaC — gives you a second opinion alongside Checkov
      - name: Trivy — Terraform IaC scan
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: config
          scan-ref: terraform/
          format: sarif
          output: trivy-iac.sarif
          severity: CRITICAL,HIGH
          exit-code: "0"        # warn only for IaC — Checkov is the enforcer
        if: hashFiles('terraform/**') != ''

      - name: Upload IaC SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: trivy-iac.sarif
          category: trivy-iac

  # ================================================================
  # JOB 4 — OWASP DEPENDENCY CHECK
  # ================================================================
  owasp:
    name: "OWASP Dependency Check"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # ADVANCED: Cache the NVD database download — it's ~300MB
      # Without this cache, every run downloads the full CVE database
      # which takes 3-5 minutes. With cache: ~15 seconds.
      - name: Cache OWASP NVD database
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2/repository/org/owasp
          key: owasp-nvd-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}-${{ github.run_number }}
          restore-keys: |
            owasp-nvd-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}-
            owasp-nvd-

      - name: Set up JDK
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
          distribution: temurin

      - name: Cache Maven
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2/repository
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-maven-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-maven-

      # ADVANCED: Run OWASP with NVD API key for faster CVE updates
      # Without API key: rate-limited to 1 request/6s (slow)
      # With API key: 50 requests/30s (fast)
      # Get free key at: https://nvd.nist.gov/developers/request-an-api-key
      - name: Run OWASP Dependency Check
        run: |
          mvn org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:check \
            -DfailBuildOnCVSS=7 \
            -DsuppressionFile=.github/owasp-suppressions.xml \
            -Dformats=HTML,SARIF,JSON \
            -DoutputDirectory=target/dependency-check \
            -DnvdApiKey=${{ secrets.NVD_API_KEY }} \
            -DautoUpdate=true

      - name: Upload OWASP SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: target/dependency-check/dependency-check-report.sarif
          category: owasp-dependency-check

      # Keep full HTML report as artifact — engineers can open it locally
      - name: Upload OWASP HTML report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: owasp-dependency-check-report
          path: target/dependency-check/
          retention-days: 30

  # ================================================================
  # JOB 5 — TFLINT (Terraform linting)
  # Only runs when terraform/ files change
  # ================================================================
  tflint:
    name: "TFLint"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    # ADVANCED: Path-based job filtering
    # This is a workaround — GitHub doesn't natively support
    # per-job path filtering. We use dorny/paths-filter to check
    # if terraform files changed and gate the rest of the job on it.
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Check if any terraform files changed
      - name: Check for Terraform changes
        id: tf-changes
        uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        with:
          filters: |
            terraform:
              - 'terraform/**'
              - '**.tf'
              - '**.tfvars'

      # ADVANCED: TFLint with AWS ruleset
      # Catches: deprecated resources, invalid instance types,
      # missing required fields, naming convention violations
      - name: Setup TFLint
        if: steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule'
        uses: terraform-linters/setup-tflint@v4
        with:
          tflint_version: v0.50.3

      - name: Initialize TFLint (downloads AWS plugin)
        if: steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule'
        working-directory: terraform
        run: tflint --init
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      # ADVANCED: Run TFLint recursively across all modules
      # --recursive scans root + all subdirectories (modules)
      - name: Run TFLint
        if: steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule'
        working-directory: terraform
        run: |
          tflint \
            --recursive \
            --config=.tflint.hcl \
            --format=sarif > ../tflint-results.sarif || TFLINT_EXIT=$?

          # Also output human-readable format to logs
          tflint --recursive --config=.tflint.hcl || true

          exit ${TFLINT_EXIT:-0}

      - name: Upload TFLint SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always() && (steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule')
        with:
          sarif_file: tflint-results.sarif
          category: tflint

  # ================================================================
  # JOB 6 — CHECKOV (IaC security + compliance)
  # ================================================================
  checkov:
    name: "Checkov IaC Scan"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      contents: read
      security-events: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check for Terraform changes
        id: tf-changes
        uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        with:
          filters: |
            terraform:
              - 'terraform/**'
              - '**.tf'
              - '**.tfvars'

      # ADVANCED: Checkov scans Terraform for 1000+ security checks:
      # - Encryption at rest/transit
      # - Public access settings
      # - Logging enabled
      # - IAM least-privilege
      # - Multi-AZ / backup settings
      # - CIS AWS benchmark compliance
      - name: Run Checkov
        if: steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule'
        uses: bridgecrewio/checkov-action@master
        with:
          directory: terraform/
          framework: terraform
          output_format: sarif
          output_file_path: checkov-results.sarif
          # ADVANCED: baseline file skips known accepted risks
          # New violations still block; baseline items are documented exceptions
          baseline: terraform/.checkov.baseline
          # These checks are intentionally skipped with justification:
          skip_check: >-
            CKV_AWS_144,
            CKV2_AWS_62
          # CKV_AWS_144: S3 cross-region replication — overkill for dev
          # CKV2_AWS_62: S3 event notifications — not needed for tfstate bucket
          soft_fail: false      # HARD FAIL — blocks pipeline on new violations
          download_external_modules: true

      - name: Upload Checkov SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always() && (steps.tf-changes.outputs.terraform == 'true' || github.event_name == 'schedule')
        with:
          sarif_file: checkov-results.sarif
          category: checkov

      # ADVANCED: Also check the Dockerfile with Checkov
      - name: Checkov — Dockerfile scan
        uses: bridgecrewio/checkov-action@master
        with:
          directory: .
          framework: dockerfile
          output_format: sarif
          output_file_path: checkov-docker.sarif
          soft_fail: true       # warn on Dockerfile, don't block (Trivy already blocks)

      - name: Upload Dockerfile Checkov SARIF
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: checkov-docker.sarif
          category: checkov-dockerfile

  # ================================================================
  # FINAL GATE JOB
  # ADVANCED: This job aggregates all scan results.
  # Nothing deploys unless every scanner passed.
  # ================================================================
  security-gate:
    name: "Security Gate"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: always()
    needs:
      - semgrep
      - sonarqube
      - trivy
      - owasp
      - tflint
      - checkov

    steps:
      - name: Evaluate all scan results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const results = {
              semgrep:   '${{ needs.semgrep.result }}',
              sonarqube: '${{ needs.sonarqube.result }}',
              trivy:     '${{ needs.trivy.result }}',
              owasp:     '${{ needs.owasp.result }}',
              tflint:    '${{ needs.tflint.result }}',
              checkov:   '${{ needs.checkov.result }}',
            };

            console.log("=== Security Scan Results ===");
            let allPassed = true;
            const failed = [];

            for (const [tool, result] of Object.entries(results)) {
              const icon = (result === 'success' || result === 'skipped') ? '✅' : '❌';
              console.log(`${icon} ${tool.padEnd(12)} ${result}`);
              if (result === 'failure') {
                allPassed = false;
                failed.push(tool);
              }
            }

            if (!allPassed) {
              core.setFailed(
                `Security gate FAILED. These scanners reported issues: ${failed.join(', ')}.\n` +
                `View findings: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/security/code-scanning`
              );
            } else {
              console.log("\n✅ All security scans passed — safe to deploy");
            }

      # ADVANCED: Post a security summary comment on PRs
      - name: Post security summary to PR
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const results = {
              '🔍 Semgrep SAST':      '${{ needs.semgrep.result }}',
              '📊 SonarQube':          '${{ needs.sonarqube.result }}',
              '🐳 Trivy':              '${{ needs.trivy.result }}',
              '🔒 OWASP Dep Check':   '${{ needs.owasp.result }}',
              '📋 TFLint':            '${{ needs.tflint.result }}',
              '✅ Checkov':            '${{ needs.checkov.result }}',
            };

            const rows = Object.entries(results)
              .map(([tool, result]) => {
                const icon = result === 'success' ? '✅' :
                             result === 'skipped' ? '⏭️' : '❌';
                return `| ${tool} | ${icon} ${result} |`;
              }).join('\n');

            const body = `## 🔐 Security Scan Summary\n| Scanner | Result |\n|---|---|\n${rows}\n\n` +
              `[View all findings](${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/security/code-scanning)`;

            // Delete previous security comments
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            for (const c of comments) {
              if (c.body.includes('Security Scan Summary')) {
                await github.rest.issues.deleteComment({
                  owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo,
                  comment_id: c.id
                });
              }
            }

            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number, body
            });
